title: Developmental cell programs are co-opted in inflammatory skin disease
quotes:
ILC2 (IL7R,
PTGDR2, GATA3) has the most distinct signature in our data and in existing literature

Fetal skin ILCs (IL7R+, RORC+ and KIT+)
resemble adult skin ILC3

In contrast to adult skin, the fetal skin lymphoid compartment is predominantly populated
by ILCs (Fig. 1D and 1F) between 7-10 PCW, prior to the development of the thymus, bone
marrow and spleen, where T and B lymphocytes differentiate
summary: Reynolds et al. (2021) identify ILC2 (IL7R, PTGDR2, GATA3) and ILC1/3 as distinct adult skin innate lymphoid clusters. ILC2_3 in Gopee 2024 combines these two populations. Fetal skin ILCs resemble adult ILC3 and predominate before thymic T cell development.

title: Natural killer cells in skin: a unique opportunity to better characterize the many facets of an overlooked secondary lymphoid organ
quotes: All three ILC subsets are present in healthy human skin; however, they are differentially expressed in disease-specific states (e.g., ILC3s are significantly increased in lesional psoriasis)
In healthy human and murine skin, most effector ILCs are of the ILC2 subset, which produces IL-5 and IL-13 and are increased in cutaneous inflammatory disease
summary: Johnson and Lee (2025) confirm all three ILC subsets in healthy human skin, with ILC2 predominating among effectors. ILC3 increases in psoriasis, and ILC2 increases in other inflammatory diseases.

title: Characterization of innate lymphoid cells (ILC) in human skin and blood demonstrates increase of NKp44+ ILC3 in psoriasis
quotes: Characterization of innate lymphoid cells (ILC) in human skin and blood demonstrates increase of NKp44+ ILC3 in psoriasis
summary: Villanova et al. (2014) are among the first to characterise ILCs in human skin, demonstrating that all three subsets are present and that ILC3 increases in psoriasis.
